key_highlights: Geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran near the Strait of Hormuz dominate markets, with oil prices swinging violently on war fears and peace deal hopes.
Oil market is described as 'completely broken' amid extreme volatility, with potential for $90 or $150 per barrel depending on escalation.
DOJ is investigating suspicious oil futures and prediction market trades timed just before major Iran war news, indicating potential insider trading.
Stock markets initially surged on Iran-deal hopes but slipped after fresh clashes, with Dow Jones hitting highs then retreating.
IonQ reported blowout earnings but stock fell due to valuation concerns and aggressive Nvidia comparison; Super Micro doubled revenue but stock flat, signaling tech rotation.
Gold rose as safe-haven buying countered Hormuz clash concerns; precious metals mining ETFs surged.
Whirlpool warned of 'recession-level' slump due to Iran war and tariff impacts, highlighting consumer weakness.
Jet fuel prices spiking, hurting airlines like British Airways owner and Spirit Airlines (which apologized after shock shutdown).
Eli Lilly, Merck, Amazon, GM, and Palantir all reported strong earnings, with price target upgrades.
Gundlach takes longshot bet on US debt restructuring, signaling deep concern about fiscal sustainability.
Trump paused Hormuz guidance effort citing progress on Iran peace deal, but also warned Iran to sign quickly or face more fire.
Macquarie profit jumped, commodities chief paid $25.5 million; private credit angst due to liquidity issues per Macquarie CFO.
China's rare earths grip remains hard to break; US targets Cuba nickel operation with China supply chain implications.
New Zealand calls for Kiwibank share sale; Indonesia foreign reserves fell to two-year low.
SpaceX sells full Colossus 1 compute to Anthropic weeks before $1.75 trillion IPO; OKX to launch pre-IPO futures for OpenAI, SpaceX, Anthropic.
Tencent and Alibaba face slowing growth as AI costs mount; Adani Energy stocks emerge as proxy for India's AI trade.
Estee Lauder plans to cut nearly 20% of jobs; Netflix downgraded from Buy to Hold.
Europe's power network gets wartime wakeup call; Australia's battery boom crowds lucrative power trade.
Goldman-backed 'Go' seeks $1.3 billion valuation in Tokyo IPO; Wealthspire to acquire $1.2 billion advisory firm.
Ted Turner, CNN founder, dies at 87.

sectors.trend: Extreme volatility with prices swinging on every headline about US-Iran clashes and peace deal prospects. Oil market is 'completely broken' per Bloomberg. Jet fuel prices spiking, hurting airlines. DOJ investigating suspicious trades.
sectors.implications: Airlines, shipping, and any fuel-intensive industries face margin pressure. Energy stocks benefit from high prices but face regulatory risk. Investors should prepare for binary outcomes: $90 oil on truce, $150 on escalation.

sectors.trend: Mixed: IonQ fell despite blowout earnings due to valuation; Super Micro doubled revenue but stock flat; AMD surged 16% on earnings; Palantir record revenue; Arm carving lucrative CPU path. AI costs mounting for Tencent/Alibaba. SpaceX pre-IPO compute deal with Anthropic.
sectors.implications: AI infrastructure spending continues but investors are becoming more discerning about valuations. Pre-IPO AI companies (SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic) attracting significant capital. Cloud earnings strong (Amazon, Alphabet).

sectors.trend: Whirlpool warns of 'recession-level' slump due to Iran war and tariffs. Estee Lauder cutting nearly 20% of jobs. McDonald's about to report earnings. Parag Milk Foods and CarTrade Tech show some resilience in India.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary names face headwinds from inflation, geopolitical uncertainty, and potential recession. Defensive consumer staples may outperform. Watch for earnings warnings.

sectors.trend: Gold rises on safe-haven buying despite Hormuz clash concerns. Gold and silver mining ETFs surge. Sugar prices sink on ethanol weakness. China's rare earths grip hard to break. US targets Cuba nickel operation.
sectors.implications: Precious metals are a key hedge against geopolitical risk and potential currency debasement. Mining stocks offer leveraged exposure. Rare earth and critical mineral supply chains remain geopolitical flashpoints.

sectors.trend: Jet fuel prices spiking, British Airways owner sees profit hit. Spirit Airlines apologizes after shock shutdown. Trimble sees freight recovery signs. Shipping sector attracts Nomura.
sectors.implications: Airlines face significant cost headwinds; some may need to raise fares or cut capacity. Freight and shipping may benefit from supply chain disruptions. Watch for bankruptcies or consolidation.

sectors.trend: Deutsche Bank beats Q1 estimates, reaffirms guidance. Macquarie profit jumps, commodities chief paid $25.5M. Private credit angst due to liquidity issues. Goldman-backed 'Go' seeks $1.3B valuation in Tokyo IPO. Wealthspire acquires $1.2B advisory firm. Blue Owl Capital sees favorable trends.
sectors.implications: Banks with strong trading desks benefit from volatility. Private credit faces liquidity scrutiny. M&A and IPO activity picking up. Emerging market bonds expected to have strong year per BlackRock.

sectors.trend: Eli Lilly remains a Buy after earnings. Merck price target raised by $1 after beating Q1 estimates. Cigna price target boosted. Cardinal Health beats estimates. Haleon outlook positive.
sectors.implications: Healthcare remains a defensive sector with strong earnings. Drug pricing and regulatory risks persist but fundamentals solid. Aging demographics support long-term demand.

sectors.trend: Starwood faces default on $265M hotel portfolio loan. Ryan Serhant helps Boston mom find home. Rent/Buy/Fix Up tips. 90s TV star's rundown home sells for $855k. Bargain auction after tenants torched rental.
sectors.implications: Commercial real estate stress continues, especially hotels. Residential market mixed with high rates. Affordability remains a challenge. Distressed opportunities emerging.

sectors.trend: Iran war risks hit Indian stocks harder. Indonesia foreign reserves fall to two-year low. Pakistan shuns spot LNG betting on Hormuz easing. ChinaAMC to accelerate tokenization. Adani Energy stocks emerge as proxy for India's AI trade.
sectors.implications: Emerging markets vulnerable to oil price shocks and geopolitical spillovers. India's AI theme gaining traction. Indonesia's reserve decline raises currency risk. China's rare earth dominance persists.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk premium: US-Iran conflict near Hormuz is the dominant market driver, causing extreme oil volatility and safe-haven flows.
Insider trading investigation: DOJ probing suspicious oil futures and prediction market trades ahead of Iran war news, raising governance concerns.
AI infrastructure boom continues but valuation scrutiny intensifies: IonQ, Super Micro, AMD, Palantir all show strong growth but market rewards selectively.
Consumer weakness emerging: Whirlpool recession warning, Estee Lauder job cuts, Spirit Airlines shutdown signal demand softening.
Central bank divergence: Japan may have sold Treasuries to intervene in yen; New Zealand calls for Kiwibank share sale; Indonesia reserves falling.
Debt sustainability fears: Gundlach bets on US debt restructuring; Trump admin proposes semi-annual earnings reporting, reducing transparency.
Pre-IPO frenzy: SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic attracting massive capital; OKX launching pre-IPO futures; Goldman-backed Go seeking $1.3B valuation.
Supply chain reshoring and critical minerals: US targets Cuba nickel; China rare earth grip; Europe power network wartime wakeup.
Private credit liquidity concerns: Macquarie CFO flags angst; Starwood default highlights CRE stress.
Earnings season divergence: Tech and healthcare strong; consumer and airlines weak; financials mixed.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: US-Iran military escalation and Hormuz blockade
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase cash and gold holdings; reduce exposure to oil-sensitive sectors (airlines, shipping, chemicals); consider long oil positions or energy ETFs as hedge; monitor diplomatic developments closely.
Diversify away from fuel-intensive industries; consider volatility strategies (options, VIX); maintain energy exposure but with tight stop-losses; hedge jet fuel costs for airlines.
Avoid trading on non-public information; review compliance procedures; be cautious with oil and prediction market positions; expect increased market surveillance.
Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary; favor defensive sectors (healthcare, utilities, consumer staples); monitor retail sales and consumer confidence data.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: US debt restructuring or default concerns
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify away from long-duration US Treasuries; consider gold, Bitcoin, or foreign bonds; monitor Gundlach's bets and debt ceiling debates.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Private credit and CRE liquidity crisis
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to private credit funds; avoid highly leveraged CRE names; favor well-capitalized banks; monitor Starwood default and Macquarie CFO comments.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Emerging market currency and reserve risks
risk_assessment.mitigation: Hedge EM currency exposure; favor EM bonds with strong fundamentals; monitor Indonesia reserves, Pakistan LNG decisions, and Indian stock vulnerability.
Take profits on high-flying AI names with extreme valuations; rotate to AI infrastructure plays with strong earnings; use options to hedge downside.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Supply chain disruption from geopolitical tensions
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify supply chains; invest in onshoring beneficiaries; monitor rare earth, nickel, and semiconductor supply risks.
Monitor Trump admin proposal for semi-annual earnings; prepare for potential reduced transparency; focus on quality of disclosures.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to gold and precious metals mining ETFs
opportunities.rationale: Geopolitical tensions, potential Fed pivot, and currency debasement fears support gold. Mining ETFs offer leveraged upside. Gold rising despite Hormuz clash shows strong buying interest.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy energy stocks with strong balance sheets and hedging capabilities
opportunities.rationale: Oil price volatility benefits integrated majors and traders. Macquarie's commodities chief paid $25.5M shows profitability. Energy sector offers both upside and hedge against escalation.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate AI infrastructure plays with strong earnings (AMD, Palantir, Arm)
opportunities.rationale: Despite valuation concerns, these companies show robust revenue growth and strategic positioning. AMD surged 16% on earnings; Palantir record revenue; Arm carving lucrative CPU path. Dip-buying opportunity.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in healthcare/pharma defensive names (Eli Lilly, Merck, Cigna)
opportunities.rationale: Strong earnings beats, price target upgrades, and defensive characteristics in uncertain macro environment. Eli Lilly remains Buy; Merck and Cigna beat estimates.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider pre-IPO exposure via OKX futures or direct allocations (SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic)
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX selling compute to Anthropic weeks before $1.75T IPO signals strong demand. OKX launching perpetual futures. High risk but potentially high reward for speculative capital.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy emerging market bonds per BlackRock recommendation
opportunities.rationale: BlackRock says EM bonds headed for strong year. Higher yields and potential Fed easing support. Diversification from developed market risks.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in shipping and freight recovery plays (Trimble, Nomura shipping interest)
opportunities.rationale: Trimble sees freight recovery signs. Nomura drawn to shipping sector. Supply chain disruptions may boost rates. Cyclical recovery play.

defensive_moves.rationale: Extreme oil volatility, geopolitical uncertainty, and potential recession warrant higher cash allocation. Short-duration bonds reduce duration risk while providing yield.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge oil exposure with put options or inverse oil ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil market 'completely broken' with binary outcomes. Protect portfolios from sudden price spikes that hurt consumer and industrial stocks. Inverse oil ETFs can hedge long energy positions.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and airlines
defensive_moves.rationale: Whirlpool recession warning, Spirit Airlines shutdown, Estee Lauder job cuts signal demand weakness. Jet fuel costs spiking. Avoid until macro clarity improves.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid private credit and highly leveraged CRE names
defensive_moves.rationale: Macquarie CFO flags liquidity angst; Starwood default on $265M hotel loan. Private credit faces redemption pressures. Stick to liquid, regulated assets.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Diversify away from US-centric portfolios with international exposure
defensive_moves.rationale: US debt restructuring bets (Gundlach), Japan Treasury sales, and EM opportunities suggest diversifying geographically. Europe power network investment, India AI trade, and New Zealand bank sale offer alternatives.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Implement tail-risk hedges (VIX calls, long-dated puts)
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical black swan risk remains elevated. Low-cost tail hedges can protect against sudden market crashes. VIX may spike on any Hormuz escalation.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets will remain highly volatile driven by US-Iran negotiations and military clashes. Oil prices will swing between $90 and $150 depending on diplomatic progress. Stock markets may rally on any peace deal but face headwinds from consumer weakness and elevated input costs. Gold and precious metals will continue to attract safe-haven flows. Tech earnings season shows strength but valuations are stretched. The DOJ investigation adds regulatory uncertainty. Expect choppy trading with a defensive tilt.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: The resolution of the Iran conflict is the key variable. A truce could unleash a relief rally in equities, especially airlines, consumer, and EM. Escalation would push oil above $150, trigger a global recession, and benefit energy, gold, and defense stocks. AI infrastructure spending will remain a multi-year growth driver but with increasing scrutiny on profitability. Central bank policies will diverge: Fed may cut if recession hits, while EM central banks face inflation pressures. Debt sustainability concerns (US, Japan, Indonesia) will keep long-term bond yields elevated. Private credit and CRE face restructuring risks. The pre-IPO boom (SpaceX, OpenAI) could create new investment opportunities.
key_catalysts: US-Iran peace deal or further military escalation
Oil price movement and potential strategic reserve releases
Fed and other central bank policy decisions
Q2 earnings season and corporate guidance
US debt ceiling negotiations and Treasury issuance
China economic stimulus and AI regulatory developments
Japan FX intervention and Treasury sales
SpaceX IPO and other high-profile pre-IPO listings
Consumer spending data and recession signals
Emerging market capital flows and currency stability
watch_list: Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic and military activity
Weekly oil inventory data and gasoline prices
US 10-year Treasury yield and yield curve
Gold price and mining ETF flows
Earnings reports from McDonald's, Nvidia, and other key names
Japan Ministry of Finance intervention data
Gundlach's debt restructuring bets and market reaction
Pre-IPO valuations and secondary market trading
Emerging market bond spreads and currency performance
Global PMI and industrial production data
